user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Wide World Importers.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 11:56 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with forecast prep. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 14:29 EST
Subject: Re: forecast prep Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with forecast prep. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 13:52 EST
Subject: forecast prep - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with forecast prep. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-01-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Wide World Importers)

[00:02:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:06:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:09:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] David (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:18:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:22:00] David (Wide World Importers): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:26:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:29:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:33:00] David (Wide World Importers): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:36:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Wide World Importers: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
